scenes.title: The World of Paul
scenes.description: A world under Roman rule. Paul writes about submission but also eschatological reversal through kenosis.
scenes.dialogue: Paul: 'Let every person be subject to the governing authorities; for there is no authority except from God.'

scenes.title: Antioch of Chrysostom
scenes.description: The rhetorical podium where speech becomes servant of theology. The city as crossroads of cultures and powers.
scenes.dialogue: Chrysostom: 'It is not to preach, but to be preached, that makes the gospel powerful.'

scenes.title: The Court of Constantinople
scenes.description: The center of political expediency. Here the bishop's parrhesia is tested against imperial power.
scenes.dialogue: Chrysostom: 'I fear neither angel nor archangel; only God.'

scenes.title: The Prison of the Spirit
scenes.description: The symbolic cell where martyrdom and persecution reveal the true nature of the Church.
scenes.dialogue: Voice of martyr: 'The blood of martyrs is the seed of Christians.'
